compiled_state.title: [claude-safe]: auto-cleanup triggered at >=10 worktrees -> worktree-cleanup.sh hangs with no output
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

When running `claude-safe` from the main repo, the script reaches the "Running automatic cleanup..." message and hangs with no further output.

## Observed Behavior

- Triggered when WORKTREE_COUNT >= 10 in `plugins/dso/scripts/worktree-create.sh:131`
- Current repo has 22 worktrees matching `worktree-` pattern (git worktree list output)
- The hang occurs at `worktree-cleanup.sh --all --force --include-branches >&2` invocation (line 136)
- The 'Running automatic cleanup...' message is emitted; no subsequent output

## Expected Behavior

Automatic cleanup should complete in reasonable time with progress output, OR emit a clear status before long operations so the user knows the script is working.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral — script hang is a functional defect in the cleanup dispatch path. Score: severity=1 (medium; blocks automatic cleanup UX), complexity=1 (moderate; involves subprocess invocation and large worktree set), environment=0 (local), flaky=0 (deterministic). Total=2 → BASIC tier.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.

Gate 3.5 (hypothesis validation): PASSED — hypothesis B confirmed with empirical evidence (timeout 15 bash -x trace showed 8/25 worktrees processed, 94528 trace lines, last line blocked on lsof -p <pid> -d cwd). Hypothesis directly explains ROOT_CAUSE.

ROOT_CAUSE: worktree-cleanup.sh performs ~275 lsof -p calls plus ~25 git ls-remote calls in a silent gather phase (is_claude_active scans all Claude pids × all worktrees) before emitting output; with 25 worktrees on this machine this takes 40-60s and the user perceives it as a hang.

Two fix options identified — proceeding to user approval per Step 4 (multiple competing fixes of comparable confidence/risk).
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Re-investigation with longer timeout (user request):

TIMED DIRECT RUN: worktree-cleanup.sh --all --force --include-branches completed in 43.0 seconds on this machine with 22 worktrees + 11 concurrent Claude pids. safe_count=0 at the end → 'No worktrees are safe to remove.' emitted and clean exit rc=0.

SILENT PHASE BREAKDOWN (lines 385-458, gather loop):
- is_claude_active: 11 pids × 2 lsof calls × 22 worktrees = ~484 lsof calls
- has_unpushed_commits: git ls-remote --exit-code origin <branch> per worktree without upstream = ~22 network calls × ~0.3s
- No progress output is emitted until the entire gather completes, then the summary table is printed all at once.

User machine exhibits 120s+ because lsof calls are especially expensive for Claude processes holding many open files on a busier system, and may retry under load.

Updated ROOT_CAUSE: worktree-cleanup.sh performs its entire worktree scan and state evaluation silently before emitting ANY output. On this machine the silent phase is 43s; on busier machines it can exceed 120s. The user reasonably interprets this as a hang and kills the process.

Hypothesis B (slow silent gather) CONFIRMED via direct timed run. Hypothesis C (network blocker) contributes but is not the dominant cost (22 ls-remote calls ≈ 7s of 43s total).
comments.author: Test
